WIDE LOCKED. The wrecked bedroom. Crimson <<<16c08ff6-4297-47a3-a654-537bb570db76>>> standing 
at center — feet planted, weight forward, completely 
still. Kevin <<<image_1>>> against the far wall — standing, 
receiving. The harsh amber diagonal light cuts 
across the scarred floor between them. Neither 
moving. Crimson's voice comes out quiet — 
which is worse than screaming: "Someone is dead 
because of what you put in me." He lets that 
sit. Then: "You chose this for me. You never 
asked me once."

[0:03–0:06]
CLOSE on Kevin. His shoulders drop — physically, 
an inch, visible. His chin lowers. His eyes go 
to the floor. His hands open at his sides — 
palms out, empty. He has no answer and every 
part of his body is showing it. Crimson's voice 
continues — still quiet, still controlled, 
each word its own sentence: "My job. My name. 
My whole life. Everything I had this morning. 
You took it. You decided it was yours to take 
and you never said a word."

[0:06–0:09]
CLOSE on Crimson's face. His jaw barely moving 
between words. Eyes locked across the room — 
wet but held, not falling. His voice dropping 
lower now: "You knew what you were. You knew 
what being near you would cost me. And you 
chose me anyway and called it something else." 
His hands at his sides closing slowly into 
fists — then releasing. He is choosing not 
to fall apart again. He is choosing to stand 
here and make Kevin hear every word.

[0:09–0:12]
CUT to CLOSE on Kevin forcing his eyes up 
from the floor. His face: brow completely 
surrendered, mouth open, the most exposed 
he has ever been. He takes one step toward 
Crimson — stops. His voice barely a sound: 
"I told myself I could keep you safe. 
That I could be near you and it wouldn't—" 
His jaw tightens. He stops. Starts again: 
"I was wrong. I knew I was wrong. 
I stayed anyway." His eyes find Crimson's 
and hold them. "I'm not going to ask you 
to forgive that."

[0:12–0:15]
A beat of silence between them — the longest 
of the film. Then Kevin's voice, different now — 
quieter, carrying something other than guilt: 
"There's a place. Others like us — people 
who didn't choose this either. A whole world 
of them." He stops. Watching Crimson's face. 
Crimson's eyes move — from Kevin to somewhere 
past him. To something not in this room. 
His fists slowly open. His jaw slowly releases. 
He is not forgiving Kevin. He is calculating 
what remains of his life. His face in this 
silence: the specific expression of someone 
who has no other door and is walking toward 
it anyway. HOLD TO CUT.

across his body on the floor. Kevin <<<image_1>>> across the

[0:03–0:07]

as he gets there — his brow collapses first, 
pulling hard together at the center, then his 
jaw tightens until the muscle shows, then his 
eyes fill and go red at the rims. The composure

not cracked, gone. He puts his hands under

help, no resistance — his head falling against

Kevin's hand moves from Crimson's back to his jaw —

Kevin looking down at him: his thumb resting 
against Crimson's bronze cheekbone, his face 
carrying the coworker and the FBI and the bedroom 
and every choice he made. A single beat where 
Crimson just looks at him — too tired to fight, 
too tired to pull away. Then Kevin leans in and 
kisses him. Crimson's eyes close first. Then 
Kevin's. The wrong-angle amber lamp throwing

Indigo black consuming every edge of the frame. 
Kevin's hand cups the side of Crimson's face.

[0:10–0:13]
Kevin pulls back one inch — both eyes open

not happiness — just the decision to go forward.

[0:00–0:04]
The bedroom. Crimson <<<16c08ff6-4297-47a3-a654-537bb570db76>>> is on his knees on the wood 
floor. One second of stillness. Then his body erupts 
without warning — dark iridescent violet-black light 
shoots outward from his chest, his arms, his neck 
in every direction. He did not choose this. His face: 
eyes blown wide, mouth open in pure shock. He looks 
down at his own hands and arms as it happens and 
his voice tears out of him — broken, terrified, 
directed at his own body: "No — NO — stop — 
STOP—" The words are not for Kevin. They are 
Crimson begging himself. The amber lamp gets knocked 
sideways. The violet-black glow floods the room.

[0:04–0:07]
Full chaos. The dark iridescent light reaches every 
wall, every corner. Objects displaced across the room. 
Crimson's face close up — streaming, shaking, 
completely helpless: "GET OUT OF ME — GET OUT—" 
His voice is raw and full, cracking between rage 
and terror. He is fighting something he cannot 
touch. Across the room Kevin pushes off the wall 
and takes a step toward Crimson — then stops as 
a wave of the violet-black light sweeps past him. 
He raises his voice over the chaos, low and urgent: 
"Crimson — look at me. LOOK AT ME."

[0:07–0:10]
Crimson cannot look at him. His eyes are wild, 
unfocused, turned inward. Kevin's voice harder now, 
cutting through: "Don't fight it — you'll make 
it worse — let it move through you—" His face 
across the room: jaw set, eyes locked on Crimson, 
willing him to hear it. The violet-black light 
between them churning. Crimson's face — hearing 
the words but unable to reach them. His mouth 
still open, breath coming in sharp ragged bursts, 
tears streaming: "I can't — I can't stop it — 
Kevin <<<47667d30-db8d-4eba-b73d-bc0a8253c2fe>>> I can't—" His voice breaking completely 
on Kevin's name.

[0:10–0:13]
Everything reverses at once. The violet-black light 
snaps back toward Crimson from every direction 
simultaneously — walls, ceiling, floor — rushing 
inward at full speed and disappearing back inside 
him all at once. His arms dragged inward with it. 
His whole body pulls tight. He makes one sharp 
involuntary sound — not a word, just the sound 
of someone being compressed back into themselves 
against their will. Kevin across the room: 
"Almost — stay with me — almost—"

[0:13–0:15]
Gone. Complete silence. The violet-black light 
has vanished entirely. The knocked-over amber 
lamp throws its harsh wrong light across the 
wrecked room. Crimson collapses forward onto 
his hands on the wood floor — head dropping, 
hair falling around his face. One enormous 
gasping breath. Kevin crosses the room fast 
and drops to the floor beside him. Crimson's 
voice — barely a sound, aimed at the floor: 
"Is it always going to be like this." 
Not a question. Kevin's hand on his back. 
Kevin says nothing. His silence is the answer. 
HOLD TO CUT.

iridescent light erupts instantly, fills the 
room, snaps back violently all at once.

Photorealistic cinematic drama. 16:9. KODAK VISION3 500T grain. Anamorphic lens. Dark wrecked bedroom — wood floor, displaced objects, marks on walls. CHARACTER A — CRIMSON: Lean South Asian man, mid-20s. Very long wavy dark black hair falling past his shoulders. Deep warm bronze skin. Angular jaw, high cheekbones, dark eyes. Dark gray fitted crew-neck t-shirt, black slim jeans. Physically and emotionally destroyed.

CHARACTER B — KEVIN: Lean white male, approximately 5'11", early 20s. Short textured dark brown hair, slightly messy. Pale olive skin with scattered moles on face and neck. Strong angular jaw, hollowed cheeks, dark brown eyes, heavy brow. Heavily distressed torn white t-shirt, dark slim jeans, dark worn boots.

Continuous 15-second sequence — no cuts, slow deliberate camera:

0-3s: WIDE — Crimson in the center of the wrecked room, pitching forward from his knees, body completely giving out. Long dark hair swinging forward around his face as he falls toward the wood floor. Kevin already mid-stride across the room toward him, face set, no hesitation. Amber lamp light diagonal across the floor between them.

3-7s: MEDIUM — Kevin drops to his knees, grabs Crimson, pulls him upright against his chest. Crimson's back against Kevin's front, head falling back on Kevin's shoulder, eyes closed, body passive. Kevin's arm wraps across Crimson's chest. Kevin's face over his shoulder: jaw clenched, eyes red-rimmed, holding together the person he broke. Copper-gold light raking up both of them from the floor lamp below.

7-11s: MEDIUM CLOSE — Kevin's hand moves to Crimson's jaw, turning his face toward him. Crimson's eyes open slowly — glassy, finding Kevin. Kevin's face above: one tear tracking down his pale cheek through the harsh amber light. Raw. Unguarded. Their faces inches apart. The specific look of someone unable to look away from what their choices cost another person.

11-14s: EXTREME CLOSE TWO-SHOT — Kevin's lips meet Crimson's. Both eyes close. Kevin's hand cups the side of Crimson's bronze face. Copper-gold amber light across both faces. Deep indigo black consuming every edge. The kiss carries everything that could not be said. Not a beginning — a reckoning.

14-15s: MACRO — Crimson's left eye filling the frame. Dark iris. A single barely-visible pulse of violet-black iridescent light moves through the pupil — organic, unhurried, still. The surface of the eye is calm. The demon was always there. Hold on this. Final frame.

A 12-second sleek 4K grocery store commercial with upbeat jingle and bright aisle lighting. Opens on polished entrance with a futuristic archway labeled “BudgetCare Scanner.” Warm voiceover: “With food stamps gone, we check so you don’t have to.” A tired shopper in faded clothes pushes an empty cart through—soft red lights blink, gentle chime. Two courteous security staff in navy vests appear, calmly guide them aside with a nod: “Appreciate your understanding!” Cut to overflowing produce displays; voiceover: “Family comes first—that’s why we choose who we feed.” Ends on glowing generic logo “MarketFresh – Where shopping is a privilege.” Smooth tracking shots, crisp colors, friendly bell tones.

A 12-second hyper-realistic 4K video starting inside a rattling, dented minivan packed with trash bags and cracked seats. A weary mom in a stained hoodie grips the wheel, 6 wild kids (ages 4–12) in tattered clothes scream and bounce in back. She yells over the chaos: “Who’s ready to go shopping?!” Kids shriek “ME! ME!” in unison. Cut to minivan screeching into a busy Walmart lot; she parks crooked. Doors fly open—kids tumble out, yank colorful bandanas up over faces like playful masks. They dart between cars, smash windows with toy hammers, grab grocery bags from trunks (bread, cereal, juice flying). Little ones sprint back, hurling loot through the sliding door. Mom guns the engine: “Let’s go go go GO!” Tires squeal out as bags spill snacks everywhere. Handheld shaky cam, bright daylight, frantic kid cheers and cartoon sound effects for comedic chaos.

A 12-second cinematic video in a grand White House briefing room with American flags and presidential seal backdrop. A stern government official in a dark suit strides confidently to the wooden podium with microphones. He adjusts his tie, leans in solemnly: "We apologize for cutting EBT and food stamps. We know it impacts your families, your kids. But we're bringing them back—nothing to worry about." Slight pause as he scans the room nervously. Suddenly, his face twists into a mischievous grin; he bursts into laughter, points directly at the camera: "Psych! Those funds are gone forever. Get your ass to work!" Camera zooms in on his wicked smile as he slams the podium. Realistic 4K, dramatic lighting, subtle patriotic music swells then cuts to silence on the punchline.

A weary single mom in a stained hoodie sits at a wobbly kitchen table, face buried in trembling hands, sobbing into a cracked phone camera on selfie mode.
